NSAA-2016-M1-001: Find the complete set of solutions to $\frac{x}{2} - 8 < 6$
NSAA-2016-M1-002: Which one of the following is a simplification of $(\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2})^2$?
NSAA-2016-M1-003: The ratio of Q:R is 5:2 and the ratio of R:S is 3:10. Which one of the following gives the ratio Q:S in its simplest form?
NSAA-2016-M1-004: The mean age of the twenty members of a running club is exactly 28. The mean age increases by exactly 2 years when two new members join. What is the mean age of the two new members?
NSAA-2016-M1-005: A medical scanner is bought for £15 000. The value of the scanner depreciates by 20% every year. By how much has the scanner reduced in value after 2 years?
NSAA-2016-M1-006: The point A is 4km due East of the point B. The bearing of the point C from A is 330° and the bearing of C from B is 060°. Find the distance BC.
NSAA-2016-M1-007: The quantities x and y are positive. x is inversely proportional to the square root of y. When x = 8, y = 9. What is the value of y when x = 6?
NSAA-2016-M1-008: In a trapezium PQRS, the parallel sides are PQ and RS. PQ = (x − 1) cm, RS = (x + 5) cm and the vertical height QR = x cm. [DIAGRAM: trapezium PQRS with parallel sides PQ and RS and height QR, not to scale] The area of the trapezium is 120 cm². What is the length of RS?
NSAA-2016-M1-009: Make b the subject of the formula: $a=\dfrac{3b^2+1}{b^2-1}$
NSAA-2016-M1-010: A thin rectangular sheet of metal 10 m by 5 m is made into an open-ended cylinder by joining the edges PS and QR. The height of the cylinder is 10 m. [DIAGRAM: rectangular sheet 10m x 5m rolled into a cylinder of height 10m] What is the volume, in cubic metres, enclosed by this cylinder?
NSAA-2016-M1-011: Which one of the following is a simplification of the given algebraic fraction?
NSAA-2016-M1-012: During summer activities week 120 students each chose one activity from swimming, archery, and tennis. 46 of the students were girls. 36 of the students chose tennis, and $\frac23$ of these were boys; 25 girls chose swimming, and 27 students chose archery. A boy is picked at random. What is the probability that he chose swimming?
NSAA-2016-M1-013: Which one of the following expressions is equivalent to the given expression involving powers of 3, 9 and 27?
NSAA-2016-M1-014: PQRS is part of a regular polygon with n sides. The side PQ is extended to T such that PQT is a straight line. The length of RQ is the same as the length of RT. [DIAGRAM: regular polygon fragment PQRS with PQ extended to T, RQ=RT, angle QRT=x marked at R] Find an equation for n in terms of x, where x is the size of angle ∠QRT in degrees.
NSAA-2016-M1-015: In a population, $\frac35$ of the adults are overweight. The probability of an overweight adult having Type 2 diabetes is $\frac{9}{50}$; this probability is 6 times the probability of an adult who is not overweight having the disease. An adult is chosen at random from the population. What is the probability the chosen adult has Type 2 diabetes?
NSAA-2016-M1-016: The graph of $y=x^2+ax+b$ meets the straight line $y=x+1$ when $x=2$ and $x=4$. Find a and b.
NSAA-2016-M1-017: A rhombus has diagonals of length 5 cm and 3 cm. An enlargement of the rhombus has sides of length $\sqrt{68}$ cm. What is the scale factor of the enlargement?
NSAA-2016-M1-018: A straight line is drawn joining two points whose coordinates are given in terms of a constant p. What is the complete set of values of p for which the gradient of this line is finite and greater than zero?
NSAA-2016-PHY-001: In the 1920s scientists discovered that the universe is expanding. One consequence of this expansion is that the intense gamma-ray radiation that filled the early universe has now been shifted to the microwave region of the electromagnetic spectrum. Which line of the table describes how the expansion of the universe has affected the frequency and wavelength of these electromagnetic waves?
NSAA-2016-PHY-002: A nuclide $^{214}_{82}$Pb changes by radioactive decay into the nuclide $^{210}_{82}$Pb. Which combination of emissions produces this change?
NSAA-2016-PHY-003: A cylindrical copper bar X of length l has cross-sectional area A. The colder end is kept at T1, the hotter end at T2. [DIAGRAM: insulated copper bar with labelled length l, area A, end temperatures T1 and T2] The curved surface is perfectly insulated. Changes in l, A, T1 and T2 may alter the rate of conduction. In which case is the rate of conduction along the insulated bar the same as for bar X?
NSAA-2016-PHY-004: The graph shown of quantity y against quantity x represents the motion of a body. [DIAGRAM: graph with x-axis from 0 to 2.0, y-axis to 10, both in SI units; g=10 N/kg] Which two of the following could the graph represent? 1: kinetic energy against velocity for a 10 kg object in free-fall. 2: potential energy against height for a 20 kg object lifted by a constant force. 3: velocity against time for a 20 kg object accelerated by a resultant force of 100 N. 4: work done by a 5 N external force against distance moved for a 12 kg object moved at constant speed.
NSAA-2016-PHY-005: A uranium-235 nucleus can undergo fission to produce two smaller nuclei. Diagram 1: $^1_1p+^{235}_{92}U\to^{89}_{36}Kr+^{144}_{56}Ba+2^1_0n$. Diagram 2: $^1_0n+^{235}_{92}U\to^{96}_{38}Sr+^{137}_{54}Xe+3^1_0n$. Diagram 3: $^1_0n+^{235}_{92}U\to^{145}_{57}La+^{87}_{35}Br+3^1_0n$. Which of the diagrams, if any, could represent this process?
NSAA-2016-PHY-006: A circuit consists of a 5.0Ω resistor and a variable resistor connected in series with a 24V battery. The variable resistor ranges from 3.0Ω to 15Ω. The battery and wires have negligible resistance. What is the maximum power dissipated in the 5.0Ω resistor?
NSAA-2016-PHY-007: The diagram shows a 12V battery, a switch and three 30Ω resistors, with an ammeter. [DIAGRAM: circuit with 12V battery, switch, ammeter and three 30Ω resistors in a specific series/parallel arrangement] Initially the switch is open. What happens to the reading on the ammeter when the switch is closed?
NSAA-2016-PHY-008: The total power P radiated by a star is given by $P=kR^2T^4$, where R is the star's radius, T its surface temperature and k a constant. The power currently radiated by the Sun is $4.0\times10^{26}$W. Towards the end of the Sun's life its radius will increase by a factor of 100 and its surface temperature will decrease by a factor of 2. What will be the power radiated by the Sun when these changes have occurred?
NSAA-2016-PHY-009: A transverse wave travelling through a medium has a frequency of 5.0 Hz, a wavelength of 4.0 cm and an amplitude of 3.0 cm. What is the total distance travelled by a particle of the medium in one minute?
NSAA-2016-PHY-010: A motor lifts a mass of 5.0kg using a pulley system secured to the roof by a coupling. [DIAGRAM: 5.0kg mass on a rope over a pulley fixed to the roof via a coupling, force applied by a motor] The motor causes the mass to accelerate upwards at 0.80 m/s². What is the minimum tension force the coupling must withstand? (g=10 N/kg, frictionless massless pulley, massless inextensible rope.)
NSAA-2016-PHY-011: A heater is connected in series with a resistor and a 6.0V battery. The total resistance of the circuit is 15Ω. In 3.0 minutes, 180J of electrical energy is transferred in the heater. How much charge flows through the heater in 3.0 minutes, and what is the voltage across the heater?
NSAA-2016-PHY-012: A cubic block of side 10cm has a square-cross-section hole of side 5.0cm through it. [DIAGRAM: 10cm cube with a 5cm square hole through its centre] The weight of the block is 30N. What is the density of the material? (g=10N/kg.)
NSAA-2016-PHY-013: Four identical solid steel balls P, Q, R and S: P, R have shiny surfaces; Q, S have dull surfaces. P and Q are in a 20°C room but are currently at 30°C. R and S are in a 40°C room and are currently at 40°C. [DIAGRAM: four balls with surfaces and current temperatures marked as described] Which two balls lose thermal energy by convection, and which ball emits thermal radiation at the greatest rate?
NSAA-2016-PHY-014: The diagram shows the velocity-time graph for an object travelling in a straight line over 30s. [DIAGRAM: velocity-time graph, velocity axis from -2.0 to 8.0 m/s, time axis 0-30s] What total distance did the object travel in the 30s, how far from its starting position was it at the end, and what was its average speed over the 30s?
NSAA-2016-PHY-015: A sample of radioactive isotope X decays to isotope Y. Y has a half-life double that of X. Initially only X is present. [DIAGRAM: five candidate graphs of number of nuclei of X and Y against time] Which graph could represent how the numbers of nuclei of X and Y vary with time?
NSAA-2016-PHY-016: Bronze is a mixture of tin and copper. A sample contains 10% tin by volume. What percentage of the mass of the sample is tin? (Density of tin = Y, density of copper = X.)
NSAA-2016-PHY-017: When a stationary uranium-238 nucleus decays by alpha emission it forms a thorium-234 nucleus. The total kinetic energy produced is E. What is the kinetic energy of the alpha particle?
NSAA-2016-PHY-018: A loudspeaker emitting regular clicks sits between two buildings 128m apart; the student and loudspeaker are 48m from one building. Each click produces two echoes. The click rate is increased until each echo coincides with a new click. What is the frequency of emission when this happens? (Speed of sound = 320 m/s.)
NSAA-2016-CHEM-001: Which one of the following atoms or ions contains the same number of neutrons and electrons as $^{40}_{20}$Ca²⁺?
NSAA-2016-CHEM-002: Solid titanium oxide does not conduct electricity and cannot be electrolysed. When molten it conducts and can be electrolysed. 7.2g of titanium form for every 3.6dm³ of oxygen at rtp. Which statements, if any, are correct? 1: after electrolysis the titanium has a noble gas electron configuration. 2: when molten, delocalised electrons carry the charge. 3: the empirical formula of titanium oxide is TiO2. (Ar: Ti=48; molar gas volume=24dm³)
NSAA-2016-CHEM-003: In which, if any, of the following reactions are covalent bonds both broken and formed? 1: burning sodium in oxygen. 2: electrolysis of aqueous sodium chloride. 3: displacement of iron from iron oxide by heating with aluminium powder.
NSAA-2016-CHEM-004: In a reversible reaction, gaseous P and Q form gaseous R and S. Increasing temperature increases both rate and equilibrium yield. Increasing pressure increases rate but yield is unaffected. Which equation could represent the reaction?
NSAA-2016-CHEM-005: Analysis of 2.4g of an oxide of bromine showed it to contain 1.6g of bromine. What is the empirical formula? (Ar: Br=80, O=16)
NSAA-2016-CHEM-006: The most common ion of antimony, Sb³⁺, has 48 electrons. Antimony has two isotopes: 70 neutrons (60% abundance) and 72 neutrons (40% abundance). What is the relative atomic mass of antimony?
NSAA-2016-CHEM-007: A chromatogram was produced for 4 dyes (W, X, Y, Z) and a mixture, using filter paper and a water solvent, with distances given on the diagram. [DIAGRAM: two chromatograms — one with 4 separate dye spots at given Rf distances, one with a mixture spot compared against them] Which of the following statements, if any, are correct? 1: the concentration of dye W must be twice that of dye Z. 2: the mobile phase is the filter paper. 3: the mixture contained dyes W and Y.
NSAA-2016-CHEM-008: The graphs show results of two experiments (W and Z) for the catalytic decomposition of hydrogen peroxide (total volume of oxygen against time). [DIAGRAM: two curves, W and Z, of total O2 volume vs time — final plateau volumes differ] Assuming other conditions are constant, which option would lead to the results shown?
NSAA-2016-CHEM-009: Two of the following equations represent redox reactions: 1: Cl2 + 2KI → I2 + 2KCl. 2: Cl2 + 2e⁻ → 2Cl⁻. 3: HCl + AgNO3 → AgCl + HNO3. 4: Cl2 + H2O → HCl + HClO. Which two equations represent redox reactions?
NSAA-2016-CHEM-010: Copper reacts with concentrated nitric acid to produce copper(II) nitrate, water and compound X (contains no copper or hydrogen). The balanced equation shows 3 moles of copper producing 4 moles of water. What is the identity of compound X?
NSAA-2016-CHEM-011: During electrolysis of a saturated NaCl solution, 2.4dm³ of hydrogen gas was collected in time t at one electrode. Assuming no products dissolve, which row correctly gives the mass or volume of product collected at the given electrode in time t? (Ar: Na=23, Cl=35.5, H=1; 1 mole of gas = 24dm³ at rtp)
NSAA-2016-CHEM-012: The heat energy change for a reaction is −100 kJ/mol, and the activation energy is +150 kJ/mol. What is the activation energy for the reverse reaction?
NSAA-2016-CHEM-013: Separate tests on monoprotic acids HX (strong) and HY (weak), both 1 mol/dm³: 1: time for a Mg strip to react completely with 25cm³ of each acid. 2: volume of 1 mol/dm³ NaOH to neutralise 20cm³ of each acid. 3: electrical conductance of each acid. Which tests, considered independently, would show HX is stronger than HY?
NSAA-2016-CHEM-014: A 1.50g sample of impure anhydrous sodium carbonate (impurity unreactive) was added to excess dilute HCl. The gas released had a volume of 240cm³ at rtp. What is the mass of the impurity? (Ar: Na=23, C=12, O=16; molar gas volume=24000cm³)
NSAA-2016-CHEM-015: 0.35g of lithium metal reacts with excess water at room temperature; the gas produced is collected and its volume measured (1 mole of gas = 24.0dm³ at rtp). What is the volume of gas collected? (Ar: Li=7)
NSAA-2016-CHEM-016: Sodium sulfate was prepared by neutralising 25.0cm³ of 0.1 mol/dm³ NaOH with exactly 50.0cm³ of sulfuric acid. What is the concentration of the sulfuric acid in g/dm³? (Mr: H2SO4=98)
NSAA-2016-CHEM-017: During electrolysis of aqueous sodium sulfate: anode: $2H_2O\to O_2+4H^++4e^-$; cathode: $2H_2O+2e^-\to H_2+2OH^-$. Which deductions, if any, can be made? 1: the mole ratio of hydrogen to oxygen produced is 1:1. 2: the sodium sulfate solution becomes more concentrated as electrolysis proceeds. 3: the whole solution becomes acidic due to H⁺ at the anode.
NSAA-2016-CHEM-018: Ethanol's structure is $CH_3CH_2OH$. Given $H_2C{=}CH_2(g)+H_2O(g)\to C_2H_5OH(g)$, $\Delta H=-45$kJ/mol, and mean bond energies (kJ/mol): H–H=+436, C–C=+346, C–H=+413, O–H=+464, C=C=+611, which option correctly gives the bond energy of the C–O bond in ethanol?
NSAA-2016-BIO-001: Between 1954 and 1971 a mine in Northern Australia released excess copper into the nearby Finnis River, killing most fish. The river remains polluted, but one type of rainbow fish survives there. A student wrote: 1: this type did not die out because it adapted to the changing environment. 2: none of the other fish types showed any genetic variation. 3: the presence of copper acted as a selective pressure. Which of the student's statements could be correct?
NSAA-2016-BIO-002: The diagram shows a plant cell with four labelled arrows (1-4) representing possible directions of water movement. [DIAGRAM: plant cell with arrows 1-4 across the membrane/cell wall] Which arrows show the net movement of water molecules, by osmosis, when the cell is surrounded by a solution more concentrated than the cytoplasm?
NSAA-2016-BIO-003: A population of organisms has the following features: 1: single celled. 2: chloroplasts present. 3: cell wall present. Using this information, which type of organism could this describe?
NSAA-2016-BIO-004: A student investigated how temperature affects an enzyme in potatoes that breaks down hydrogen peroxide. Bubbles of oxygen produced in one minute: 30°C: 32; 40°C: 27; 50°C: 23; 60°C: 8; 70°C: 2. Which student correctly calculated the percentage decrease in bubbles between 30°C and 60°C, and gave an appropriate explanation?
NSAA-2016-BIO-005: Albinism is a recessive genetic condition. In a population of 580 000 people there were 29 albinos and 81 200 symptomless carriers. One cheek cell was collected from every individual. What is the number of albinism alleles in these cells?
NSAA-2016-BIO-006: A student set up apparatus with a human enzyme solution and protein solution at 25°C, pH 7; it took 15 minutes for the cloudy mixture to become clear. What could the student change so it takes less than 15 minutes?
NSAA-2016-BIO-007: Apparatus measured the distance a gas bubble moved along a capillary tube (diameter 1.0mm) in 3 minutes as 16mm, while investigating pondweed photosynthesis rate. [DIAGRAM: light source, pondweed, capillary tube with gas bubble and scale] After 3 minutes the rate of gas production is reduced. Which row gives the correct volume of gas produced and reason for the rate reduction?
NSAA-2016-BIO-008: A student used a light microscope at 40x magnification on a stained, non-dividing tissue sample. Cells showed a large central vacuole and cytoplasm containing a single stained round structure. Which structures, if any, might also have been seen at this magnification? 1: cell wall. 2: mitochondria. 3: chromosomes.
NSAA-2016-BIO-009: The diagram shows the tip of a plant shoot with two areas, P and Q. [DIAGRAM: shoot tip with regions P near the very tip and Q further back] A student wrote: 1: cells at P are smaller than cells at Q. 2: concentration of plant hormone is higher at Q than P. 3: unidirectional light has caused a change in the concentration of plant hormone at P. Which of the statements, if any, could be correct?
NSAA-2016-BIO-010: A DNA molecule was separated into strands 1 and 2; strand 1 had adenine 26%, guanine 28%, thymine 14% (cytosine unstated). P, Q, R, S are the percentages of adenine, cytosine, guanine, thymine in strand 2. A student calculated: P=14%, Q=28%, R=26%, S=28%. Which of these percentages is/are correct?
NSAA-2016-BIO-011: Cystic fibrosis is a recessive genetic condition of the respiratory system. A female carrier has a child with a male who is also a carrier. What are the probabilities that the child is (i) a male with cystic fibrosis, and (ii) a female who does not have cystic fibrosis but carries the recessive allele?
NSAA-2016-BIO-012: Organisms interact in different ways: 1: a tapeworm absorbing nutrients from a sheep intestine. 2: a bacterial cell breaking down undigested food in a human gut. 3: a leopard and lion hunting an antelope. 4: male deer fighting each other to gain a mate. 5: oak trees growing close together in a wood. Which statement(s) are examples of intraspecific competition?
NSAA-2016-BIO-013: A diploid cell from a fly with 8 chromosomes divides to form sperm cells. Which row is correct for the number of strands of DNA in each sperm cell and the number of sperm cells produced?
NSAA-2016-BIO-014: A healthy human runs 1500m as fast as possible. Which row shows molecules in higher concentration in a vein carrying blood away from an actively contracting leg muscle, compared to an artery carrying blood to that muscle's capillaries? (✓=higher, X=not higher)
NSAA-2016-BIO-015: The sex of species Q is controlled by chromosomes X and Y, but inherited in the opposite pattern to humans (Q females = XY-equivalent of human males, Q males = XX-equivalent of human females). A family tree for one population of species Q is shown. [DIAGRAM: pedigree chart with male/female symbols across generations] What is the ratio, in simplest form, of males to females, and the total number of Y chromosomes in this family tree?
NSAA-2016-BIO-016: The graph shows how oxygen concentration, algae, bacteria and fish numbers vary with distance downstream from a point where pollution entered a river, at 5 marked points. [DIAGRAM: graph with curves for oxygen, algae, bacteria, fish against distance downstream, points 1-5 marked] Which statement below can be correctly concluded from the graph?
NSAA-2016-BIO-017: The graph shows the effect of increasing substrate concentration on an enzyme-controlled reaction, other variables constant. [DIAGRAM: rate vs substrate concentration curve rising then plateauing, typical saturation curve] Which of the following labels, if any, could be correct for the y-axis? 1: rate of substrate loss (mg/min). 2: rate of enzyme-substrate complex formation (complexes/s). 3: rate of product formed per enzyme molecule (mg/min).
NSAA-2016-BIO-018: Given a DNA triplet code table, and the sequence …CGC AGT… An insertion occurs between the first and second bases; later a deletion removes one base from the sequence. Assuming this is part of a longer coding sequence and no other mutations occur, what are the probabilities that (i) after the first mutation, the first triplet does not code for arginine, and (ii) after the second mutation, both triplets code for arginine?
NSAA-2016-M2-001: When x=2 is substituted into $x^3+px^2+qx+p$ the result is 0. When x=1 is substituted into the same expression, the result is $-3.5$. Find all possible value(s) of p.
NSAA-2016-PHY-019: A parachutist falls at terminal speed with the parachute open. Forces are labelled: weight of parachutist (R), force from parachute on parachutist (P), air resistance on parachutist (Q), force from parachutist on parachute (N), weight of parachute (M), air resistance on parachute (L). [DIAGRAM: free-body diagrams of the parachute and the parachutist showing forces L, M, N, P, Q, R] Consider equations: 1: L+M=N. 2: P+Q=R. 3: L=Q. 4: N=P. 5: L+Q=R+M. Which of these, if any, is/are a direct result of applying Newton's Third Law here?
NSAA-2016-M2-002: A square PQRS is drawn above the x-axis with side PQ on the x-axis. P is (−5,0) and Q is (1,0). A circle is drawn inside the square with diameter equal to the side of the square. Which one of the following is an equation of the circle?
NSAA-2016-PHY-020: A shopper pushes a supermarket trolley 15m in a straight line on a level surface, applying a constant force of 50N at 37° below the horizontal. The total weight of the trolley and contents is 350N. (sin37°=0.60, cos37°=0.80.) What is the magnitude of the total vertical force the surface exerts on the trolley, and how much work is done by the pushing force?
NSAA-2016-M2-003: The first term of a convergent geometric series is 8. The fifth term is 2. The sixth term is real and positive. What is the sum to infinity of this series? ($S_\infty=\frac{a}{1-r}$)
NSAA-2016-PHY-021: A non-uniform plank of mass 15kg is used as a see-saw, pivoted at its centre. A boy of mass 35kg sits with his centre of gravity 1.20m from the pivot; a woman of mass 60kg balances the see-saw sitting with her centre of gravity 0.80m from the pivot on the other side. [DIAGRAM: see-saw with pivot, boy 1.20m one side, woman 0.80m other side] Where is the centre of gravity of the plank, and what is the force between the pivot and the plank? (g=10N/kg.)
NSAA-2016-M2-004: Tangents are drawn from a point P to a circle of radius 10cm; the centre is C and PC=20cm. [DIAGRAM: circle with two tangent lines from external point P, radius 10cm, PC=20cm, shaded region between tangents and arc] Which one of the following is an expression for the shaded area in square centimetres?
NSAA-2016-PHY-022: A 200kg car on a fairground ride travels at 5.0m/s at point X, then moves down a sloping track without energy input. Point X is at height 8.0m, point Y at height 2.0m; at Y its speed is 9.0m/s. [DIAGRAM: sloping track from X (height 8.0m) to Y (height 2.0m)] How much energy is transferred overcoming resistive forces from X to Y? (g=10N/kg.)
NSAA-2016-M2-005: Given that $7\cos\theta-3\tan\theta\sin\theta=1$, which one of the following is true?
NSAA-2016-PHY-023: A uniform, solid, heavy cube of side d rests with one edge on a level table. A horizontal force P acts on another edge, and the cube is stationary. [DIAGRAM: cube on a table with horizontal force P applied at the top edge] Statements: 1: it is possible there is no frictional force between cube and table. 2: there must be a frictional force acting to the left. 3: there must be a frictional force acting to the right. 4: force P has a clockwise moment about the contact edge equal to P×d. Which statement(s) are correct?
NSAA-2016-M2-006: The complete set of values of $a$ for which the equation $3x^2=(a+2)x-3$ has two real distinct roots is:
NSAA-2016-PHY-024: An object is fired vertically upwards from the ground at t=0 in still air at 8.0m/s. On the way up, what is the height of the object when it has a speed of 2.0m/s, and at what time does it reach this height on the way down? (g=10N/kg, air resistance ignored.)
NSAA-2016-M2-007: The line $y=mx+3$ (with $m>0,m\neq1$) is perpendicular to the line $y=px+2$. The lines cut the x-axis at points L and M respectively; LM=5 units. [DIAGRAM: two perpendicular lines crossing the x-axis at L and M, y-intercepts 3 and 2 respectively] What is the value of $m+p$, given $m>1$?
NSAA-2016-PHY-025: Ball P (mass 4.0kg) moves right at 10m/s directly towards stationary ball Q (mass 2.0kg). [DIAGRAM: ball P approaching stationary ball Q along a line] They collide but do not join. Immediately after, ball Q moves at 10m/s to the right. What is the velocity of ball P after the collision, and how much kinetic energy in total is lost?
NSAA-2016-M2-008: $f(x)$ is a cubic function of x involving a positive constant a. Find the complete set of values of x for which f(x) is an increasing function.
NSAA-2016-PHY-026: A 2.0kg object rests on a level surface; the coefficient of friction between object and surface is 0.25. Two horizontal forces at right angles, 9.0N and 12.0N, are applied simultaneously. What is the magnitude of the acceleration of the object as it begins to move? (g=10N/kg.)
NSAA-2016-M2-009: The curve $y=x^2$ is translated by the vector $\binom{4}{3}$ and then reflected in the line $y=-1$. Which one of the following is an equation of the resulting curve?
NSAA-2016-PHY-027: An object of mass 20kg is pulled up a rough plane inclined at 30° by a light inextensible cable over a frictionless pulley connected to a freely-falling 30kg mass. [DIAGRAM: 20kg block on 30° incline connected via pulley to hanging 30kg mass] The acceleration along the plane is 2.5m/s². What is the frictional force between the object and the plane? (g=10N/kg.)
